mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand growth across durable and non-durable sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a win rate below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index reflect contracting manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative during periods of falling INDPRO, or the mechanism yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains low, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns under high inflation and low rates show a win rate below 40% over 20-day horizons, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit zero or negative correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate dropping below 1.0 percent signals an imminent economic contraction and earnings recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0 percent, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a synchronized supply-side recovery and labor market tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 45% across multiple expansion cycles.
